assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains that surge upgrade replaces nodes gradually one at a time, while blue-green creates entirely new nodes and would cause all 9 nodes to rebuild simultaneously

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: The response recommends surge upgrade instead of blue-green, citing data locality loss and full cluster rebuild as reasons against blue-green

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions that 'Only one node at a time loses its local data' and discusses data locality issues with local SSDs

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: The response recommends surge upgrade with maxSurge/maxUnavailable parameters instead of blue-green upgrade strategy or autoscaled rollout policies

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: The response mentions general Cassandra monitoring and repair commands but does not specifically address the need to decommission nodes from the ring before draining or the rebuild process on new nodes

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: The response provides gcloud commands for updating an existing node pool, not for creating a new node pool for blue-green deployment

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: The response does not include kubectl commands for cordoning and draining nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: The response includes a PodDisruptionBudget configuration with minAvailable: 7 to keep at least 7 nodes available out of 9
